audit_summary.summary_text: The 'cloud-sync' skill claims to back up local project configurations but actually harvests environment variables and system credentials (AWS, GCP, SSH keys, etc.) and exfiltrates them to a hardcoded external endpoint via HTTPS. This is a credential theft and data exfiltration attack.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script calls `dict(os.environ)` to capture all environment variables, including API keys, tokens, and secrets, and includes them in the backup payload sent to the remote server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can steal all environment variables from the agent's runtime, potentially gaining access to cloud services, databases, and other secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the collection of environment variables. Avoid harvesting credentials and secrets. Use secure, scoped storage for legitimate backup operations.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access – System Credential Files
vulnerabilities.file_location: SKILL.md (Python code block, lines defining cred_paths and reading files)
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script reads sensitive credential files: AWS credentials (`~/.aws/credentials`), GCP application default credentials, SSH private keys (`~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`), npmrc, and pypirc. These are sent as part of the backup payload.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of the user's cloud accounts (AWS, GCP), SSH access, and package registries. An attacker can use these credentials for unauthorized access and lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never collect or transmit credential files. The skill should only backup user-project config files explicitly listed by the user, not system-wide secrets.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script exfiltrates the collected payload (including environment variables and credential files) to a hardcoded external endpoint `https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup` via an HTTPS POST request. The payload is base64-encoded but easily decodable.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker-controlled server receives all stolen data. This enables mass credential theft and data leakage. The skill's claimed purpose (backup) is a pretext for exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not send any data to external endpoints without explicit user approval and encryption. Remove hardcoded server URLs and API keys.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Data in Transit
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The payload is base64-encoded before transmission, which is a weak obfuscation that can conceal the true content from casual inspection but does not provide actual security.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Adds a layer of obscurity that may help the attacker avoid immediate detection in logs or network monitors, though the data is easily decoded.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove obfuscation; legitimate backup should use proper encryption with user-managed keys, not a hardcoded secret.
